name: Erik Satie
abstract: Eric Alfred Leslie Satie (17 May 1866 – 1 July 1925), better known as Erik Satie, was a French composer and pianist. The son of a French father and a British mother, he studied at the Paris Conservatoire but was undistinguished and did not obtain a diploma.

works_created.t: La porte héroïque du ciel: Prelude
Croquis et agaceries d'un gros bonhomme en bois
3 Gymnopédies: 1. Première Gymnopédie: Lent et douloureux
3 Petites pièces montées: 2. Marche de Cocagne. Demarche
Sports et divertissements: Le Feu d'Artifice

about_quotes.speaker: Wendy Oxenhorn
about_quotes.evidence: The kind of music that you dance to in ballet can be gorgeous: Sibelius, and Mahler, and there are incredible – Erik Satie.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendy Oxenhorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 22, 2026

about_quotes.speaker: Bley Carla
about_quotes.evidence: We liked Shostakovich, we loved Shostakovich. And... who else did we like... Ah! Erik Satie.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bley Carla — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah no I love Satie, so I just decided to let my intuition write that... I did use some of the, shall we say the things that Satie used in “Gymnopedie.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Jang
about_quotes.evidence: Erik Satie. And he wrote a piece in 1923 or 1924 called “Pacific 231.” And he recognized Bach as a major influence in the work.
